# bench: # TWO MDAM paths measured for EVERY bench, cold-amortized ns/shot (total_wall/N), taskset -c 2, single-thread BLAS.
#   auth  = authoritative path (run()/sample_batch): no cache, exact per-shot, CONSTANT. Wins via r<<k localization.
#   lean  = path-3 lean-walk (run_lean_fb_batch): skip engine gate-walk via magic-core automaton, miss->best-stack fallback.
#           cold-AMORTIZED (empty table -> fallback builds it), target N=10M, per-bench wall-cap ~30s -> lean_N.
#   speedup_X = clifft_ns / X_ns  (>1 => MDAM faster than the Clifft paper baseline). clifft_ns REUSED (constant).
#   fallback_pct = lean-miss rate = SATURATION diagnostic (fb->0 as N grows => automaton saturates; high => never saturates).
#   best_path / best_speedup = pick min(auth_ns, lean_ns) per bench = the actual best MDAM path.
# RULE: fb is a DIAGNOSTIC, not the decision (lean falls back to best-stack, not auth). Decide by min(auth_ns,lean_ns).
#   Physical predictor: localizable r<<k (small auth_ns despite big k, fb~100%) => auth; bounded magic core (fb->0) => lean.
# bit-exact = lean fallback guarantees it; spot-checked (2 seeds x 2000 shots) vs authoritative sample_batch.
